pattern: A three-dimensional interval DP carries how many same-colored boxes are attached to the left of a segment, letting the solver decide whether to clear them immediately or merge them with a later matching box for a larger square bonus.
hints: Removing a run of k equal boxes scores k squared, so it pays to gather equal colors together before removing them.
Define the value of a subrange given that some boxes of the same color as its first element are already attached on the left.
Recurse on a range [i, j] with a count of attached equal boxes; either remove the leading group now, or for each matching box inside the range, split so that the leading group merges with it.

edge_inputs.description: all same color removed at once
matching ends around a different middle

stress.description: short random sequence over a tiny color palette to encourage merges
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(40, max(1, size))
    colors = rng.randrange(2, 5)
    return [[rng.randrange(1, colors + 1) for _ in range(n)]]
stress.note: length capped so the cubic-plus DP stays well under the time limit
